title: Legend of the Fifth Sun: Nanahuatzin's Sacrifice
description: The Aztec creation myth: the humble god Nanahuatzin throws himself into the fire to become the sun.
introduction: The Aztec cosmos had been created and destroyed four times before our present age — the Fifth Sun. This narrative tells how the gods gathered at Teotihuacan and how the humble, pox-covered Nanahuatzin had the courage to leap into the sacred fire to become the sun, while the proud Tecuciztecatl hesitated. The myth establishes the Aztec theology of reciprocal sacrifice: the sun exists because a god died, and it must be sustained by human offering.

translation.original_text: In ic macuilli tonatiuh, in iuh quitoaya huehuetque.
translation.text: Concerning the fifth sun, as the elders used to say.

translation.original_text: Niman ye ic motlalitemoc Nanahuatzin. Niman ye onaci in tlecuilli, niman ye ic oncalac in tletl.
translation.text: Then Nanahuatzin ran and threw himself into the fire. He reached the great hearth and cast himself into the flames.
